title: Relationship Between Likelihood and Sum-of-Squares Functions in ARIMA Models
description: A visual comparison showing how maximizing the likelihood function is equivalent to minimizing the sum-of-squares function under the assumption of normally distributed errors, which is central to parameter estimation in ARIMA time series models.
elements: ARIMA Model with Parameters (e.g., φ, θ)
relationships: The ARIMA model generates error terms from the data and initial parameter guesses
The error terms are fed into both the Likelihood and Sum-of-Squares functions
Maximizing the Likelihood Function is mathematically equivalent to minimizing the Sum-of-Squares Function when errors are normally distributed
